an3.130:1.1: Then Ven. Anuruddha went to Ven. Sāriputta and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
an3.130:1.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to Ven. Sāriputta,
an3.130:1.3: “Here, by means of the divine eye, purified & surpassing the human, I see the thousand-fold cosmos.
an3.130:1.4: And my persistence is aroused & unsluggish. My mindfulness is established & unmuddled. My body is calm & unaroused. My mind is concentrated & gathered into singleness.
an3.130:1.5: And yet my mind is not released from the effluents through lack of clinging/sustenance.”
an3.130:2.1: Ven. Sāriputta: “My friend, when the thought occurs to you,
an3.130:2.2: ‘By means of the divine eye, purified & surpassing the human, I see the thousand-fold cosmos,’ that is related to your conceit.
an3.130:2.3: When the thought occurs to you,
an3.130:2.4: ‘My persistence is aroused & unsluggish. My mindfulness is established & unmuddled. My body is calm & unaroused. My mind is concentrated & gathered into singleness,’ that is related to your restlessness.
an3.130:2.5: When the thought occurs to you,
an3.130:2.6: ‘And yet my mind is not released from the effluents through lack of clinging/sustenance,’ that is related to your anxiety.
an3.130:2.7: It would be well if—abandoning these three qualities, not attending to these three qualities—you directed your mind to the deathless property.”
an3.130:3.1: So after that, Ven. Anuruddha—abandoning those three qualities, not attending to those three qualities—directed his mind to the deathless property.
an3.130:3.2: Dwelling alone, secluded, heedful, ardent, & resolute, he in no long time entered & remained in the unexcelled goal of the holy life for which clansmen rightly go forth from home into homelessness, directly knowing & realizing it for himself in the here & now.
an3.130:3.3: He knew, “Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for the sake of this world.”
an3.130:3.4: And thus Ven. Anuruddha became another one of the arahants.
